item.text: {"approval_state":"proposed","bottleneck":"The evidence now covers three cells of a 2x2 comparison (`working_set_kb` in {2048, 256} and `stride_bytes` in {128, 4}), but the 256 KB, 4-byte-stride control is still missing, so the workflow cannot yet distinguish a pure working-set effect from a stride-by-capacity interaction under matched launch parameters.","continue_recommended":true,"expected_gain":"One bounded follow-up would complete the minimal comparison matrix, making the next analysis materially stronger: reviewers could compare stride effects at both working-set sizes and compare working-set effects at both strides without changing benchmark family, controls, or workflow stages.","proposal":"Run one rollbackable follow-up spec in the existing `cuda_memory_hierarchy_probe` handler with the same launch shape and deterministic controls as `spec_3626106571cf`, `spec_ea7294e5fd1a`, and `spec_d89a9f30c9b4`, but set `working_set_kb=256` and `stride_bytes=4`. Analyze it only as the missing matched control for the current 2x2 design: if the 4-byte stride remains substantially faster than 128-byte stride at 256 KB and the 256 KB, 4-byte result is similar to or above the 2048 KB, 4-byte control, record a narrow claim that hostile-stride penalty persists even when the working set is reduced; if not, keep conclusions limited to observed configuration-specific bandwidth differences. Preserve append-only history, keep cache-level attribution out of curated knowledge, and treat this as a matrix-completion step rather than a new benchmark family.","proposed_profile_updates":[{"context_sections":["question","spec","claim","verification"],"instruction_note":"When a proposal is extending a controlled parameter matrix, require the spec generator to name the matrix axes, list the already-measured cells by spec id, and identify the single missing cell the next run will fill.","reason":"This makes experimental coverage explicit for human review and reduces redundant follow-up specs.","role":"spec_generator","sandbox_mode":"read-only","web_search_enabled":false},{"context_sections":["spec","claim","verification"],"instruction_note":"For memory-hierarchy analysis, permit interaction language only after both values of each declared axis have at least one matched comparison; otherwise restrict wording to single-axis or configuration-specific observations.","reason":"This preserves rigor and prevents over-interpreting partial factorial evidence.","role":"analyzer","sandbox_mode":"read-only","web_search_enabled":false}],"risk":"The added 256 KB, 4-byte result may still be noisy or may show little separation, which would complete the matrix structurally but still leave the mechanistic interpretation weak.","rollback_condition":"If the 256 KB, 4-byte-stride run cannot be executed deterministically within the 120-second budget, produces incompatible metadata, or fails independent rerun stability, keep the current accepted claims as-is and do not elevate the analysis beyond the existing partial matrix.","stop_reason":null,"target_workflow_version":"workflow_v1"}
